description:
Closed registry of disclosure rules. This is where honesty is MECHANIZED.

Every rule fires from a machine-checkable fact in the artifact — never from caller text, and never from a caller flag. That is the whole design: a caller cannot suppress a disclosure by omitting a field, cannot weaken one by rewording it, and cannot promote its data by setting a boolean. The only way to remove a disclosure is to remove the fact that causes it.

The rendered text is generated from `text` here. It appears identically in four places — the artifact JSON, the visible SVG footer, the SVG accessible description, and the table metadata — and a test asserts all four agree.

policy.orderingBySeverity: critical, then important, then informational; within a severity, by rule id. A compact footer may show the highest-priority disclosures and an overflow count; the complete in-memory table metadata and accessible description always contain ALL of them. Artifact 1.0 emits no sidecar.
policy.neverSuppressible: In stable mode no disclosure can be suppressed, reordered below a caller note, or visually dominated by one.
policy.callerNoteIsNotADisclosure: A caller's declared note is rendered separately, bidi-isolated, under an attribution that marks it unverified. It is never merged into the mandatory disclosure text.

rules.text: Simulation output. These values were produced by a model, not measured from a biological system.
Synthetic fixture. This data was fabricated to exercise the software and carries no scientific meaning whatsoever.
Source unknown. The origin of this data was not declared, so nothing about its provenance can be relied upon.
Extracted from published material. Values were transcribed from a publication and have not been re-derived from primary data.
Manually entered. These values were typed in by hand rather than exported from an instrument or simulator.

rules.trigger: library-derived sourceAuthenticityVerified == false (always false in Artifact 1.0 because it has no source-authentication input or verifier)
rules.text: Source authenticity not verified. Cortexel validated the structure and semantics of this request; it did not check that the underlying source bytes are what they claim to be.
No independent reference comparison was run for this figure.
Partial network view. This is not the complete network: absent connections here have not been shown to be absent globally.

Rank-local snapshot (rank {rank} of {worldSize}). Under MPI, a rank holds the connections whose target it owns. In-degree is therefore complete for these local targets; out-degree and global completeness are not.
Edges sampled: {retainedConnectionCount} of {sourceConnectionCount} connections are shown. Degree and completeness cannot be read from this figure.
Node set incomplete. Nodes with no drawn edge may still have connections; a node missing here is not a node with degree zero.
Multiple connections between the same endpoint pair were combined using {aggregation}. The drawn value is an aggregate, not a single synapse.
An empty cell means no connection was observed. It is distinct from a connection whose weight is zero, which is drawn as a value.

rules.trigger: graph layout is not from measured positions
rules.text: Layout is schematic. Node placement was chosen for legibility and carries no spatial meaning.

rules.trigger: spatial figures where some selected nodes have no position
rules.text: {missingCount} of {totalCount} nodes have no declared position and are omitted from the map rather than placed at the origin.
{excludedCount} observations fell outside the declared observation window and are excluded from this analysis.

rules.trigger: the event window uses the NEST origin-relative serialized clock
rules.text: NEST clock boundary — Cortexel checked origin, start, stop, and event times as the exported binary64 millisecond values. It did not inspect NEST's hidden integer-tic state or establish the source export's authenticity; pre-serialization timing remains caller-declared.
Missing observations: {missingCount}. Missing values are omitted rather than interpolated, imputed, or drawn as zero.

rules.trigger: derivation contains a unit conversion operation
rules.text: Units were converted during derivation: {conversions}. Source values remain in the canonical request embedded in the artifact; converted table values identify their units.
No uncertainty is shown ({reason}). The absence of an uncertainty mark means uncertainty was not supplied — not that it is small.

rules.trigger: at least one non-none uncertainty is declared and fewer than all displayed series have complete drawable uncertainty at every non-missing central observation
rules.text: Complete drawable uncertainty is present for {shownCount} of {seriesCount} displayed series; non-none uncertainty was declared for {declaredCount}. A missing uncertainty mark or bound means drawable uncertainty was absent there — not that it is small.

rules.trigger: response curves supplied as aggregates rather than raw repeats
rules.text: Responses were supplied as per-condition aggregates ({estimator}, n = {sampleCount}). The individual repeats were not supplied and cannot be shown.

rules.trigger: an event-derived response declares an event selection
rules.text: Event scope is caller-declared. Cortexel checks internal structure and arithmetic, but cannot establish selection referents, recorded-sender count, completeness, pooling actually performed, membership-digest preimages, or that counts, latencies, and peaks came from that selection without the source records. A single selected train may itself be pre-pooled; its source composition is not bound.

rules.trigger: an event-derived response pools one or more recorded sender trains but binds only their declared cardinality, not an identifier set
rules.text: Event-scope membership is cardinality-only. The selected-sender count is declared, but member identities are not bound; Cortexel cannot establish that the same sender population was used across conditions or repeats.
Kernel-smoothed rate ({kernel}, bandwidth {bandwidth}). This is an estimate, not a literal count per bin, and it is drawn as a continuous line rather than as steps.

rules.trigger: autocorrelation: each event's pairing with itself was excluded
rules.text: Autocorrelation: each event paired with itself is excluded. Distinct events with identical times are retained, so the zero-lag bin is not necessarily empty.
Positive lag means the target train follows the reference train by that lag.

rules.trigger: the caller supplied pre-binned rows rather than raw observations
rules.text: Input was pre-binned by the caller. Exact aggregate counts and denominators were producer-supplied: Cortexel checks their integer/range consistency and re-derives the normalization, but cannot re-derive them from raw observations. Observation identities, membership partitions, and coverage not explicitly retained by the aggregate fields cannot be recovered.

rules.trigger: the per-selected-sender PSTH normalization divides by a declared rectangular selected-sender exposure
rules.text: Rectangular selected-sender exposure was asserted. The per-selected-sender rate assumes every selected sender was observable in every counted trial and bin; Cortexel cannot verify that exposure from spike-event presence or pre-binned aggregates.
Samples sharing a timestamp were combined using {method}.

rules.trigger: one or more null replicate values were excluded from a duplicate-time aggregate that retained a non-null result
rules.text: {missingReplicateCount} missing replicate values were excluded from duplicate-time aggregates. An all-missing replicate group remains a visible gap rather than becoming zero.
The figure carries a note declared by the caller. Cortexel has not verified it.

rules.trigger: the active budget profile is not a published default
rules.text: Rendered under a non-default budget profile ({profileId}). This figure does not claim default conformance.
